<!-- ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  STEP 1 OF 2 — Pitch your idea here first.

  A maintainer will review this issue, ask questions, and let you know
  whether to proceed to Step 2: writing the full RFC document and
  opening a Pull Request using rfcs/template.md.

  You do not need to write a full RFC to open this issue.
  A clear description of the problem and rough idea is enough.
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── -->

## Summary

<!-- One paragraph: what are you proposing and why? -->

## Domain and tier impact

**Domain:**
**Tiers affected:**

## Problem this solves

<!-- What real-world gap or issue does this address?
     Evidence from field experience, evaluator feedback, or CSO input
     is strongly preferred over theoretical concerns. -->

## Proposed change (rough sketch)

<!-- You don't need the full RFC format here — a rough description is fine.
     The Working Group will help refine this if the pitch is accepted. -->

## Have you checked for existing RFCs?

- [ ] Yes — no open issue or RFC file in rfcs/ already covers this

## Regional considerations

<!-- Does this apply universally, or might it need regional adaptation?
     E.g. a criterion about legal registration looks different in Kenya vs. Germany. -->

<!-- WHAT HAPPENS NEXT
  1. A Working Group member will respond within 2 weeks.
  2. If the pitch is accepted, you'll be asked to open a PR that adds a
     completed rfcs/NNNN-your-title.md file using rfcs/template.md.
  3. That PR opens the 30-day public comment period.
  4. The Working Group votes; accepted RFCs are merged and criteria files updated.

  If you'd rather discuss informally before opening an issue, email:
  step-framework@techsoup.org
-->
